pi_items.source: setup_task.sh creates this file with hardcoded GeoJSON containing Area A and Area B features
setup_task.sh generates this 600x300 pixel GeoTIFF using GDAL with synthetic land cover data
Task description instructs agent to save zonal histogram results to this path
setup_task.sh creates GeoJSON with exactly 2 features: Area A (west polygon) and Area B (east polygon)
Task step 5 produces HIST_1 via Zonal Histogram, step 6 creates pct_water via Field Calculator
Raster generation logic: Area A (west half, columns 0-300) assigned Class 1 (Water) with 1% random Class 3 noise. Analysis shows 89082 water pixels out of 90000 total = 98.98%
Raster generation logic: Area B (east half, columns 300-600) assigned Class 2 (Forest) with 1% random Class 3 noise. Area B has no Class 1 pixels = 0% water
setup_task.sh lines 70-71: min_x=-122.5, max_x=-121.9, min_y=37.5, max_y=37.8
Task description line 3 and setup_task.sh comments lines 58-62

privileged_info_summary: The task uses synthetically generated GIS data: a 600×300 pixel GeoTIFF covering coordinates -122.5° to -121.9°W and 37.5° to 37.8°N, with land cover classes 1=Water (west half), 2=Forest (east half), and 3=Urban (1% random noise). The vector layer contains 2 features (Area A and Area B) covering the western and eastern halves respectively. Area A is expected to contain approximately 98.98% water pixels (Class 1), while Area B contains 0% water (all Class 2 Forest). The task requires computing zonal histogram statistics (HIST_1, HIST_2, HIST_3) and calculating pct_water as (HIST_1 / total) * 100.
